mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates above 5% increase the discount rate for equity cash flows, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of co-occurring positive growth instances, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index values above the 2017 baseline of 100 reflect expanded manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of observations when INDPRO exceeds 103.0, or the signal loses predictive power during inventory accumulation phases.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of federal funds rates above 5% and CPIAUCSL exceeding 310 creates a stagflationary environment that compresses equity valuations and earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when both thresholds are met, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from independent main effects in nested model tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in chained dollars GDP directly translates to corporate revenue expansion and earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative direction in >50% of positive GDP growth quarters, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 over 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A single-month nonfarm payroll increase exceeding 200,000 persons demonstrates robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in out-of-sample walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling retail sales indicates unsold inventory accumulation and weakening consumer demand, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of divergence instances, or the predictive relationship reverses during supply-constrained periods.
